description_long: This is a long-handled shovel featuring a straight, cylindrical shaft with a dark, textured grip at the top. The handle appears to be made of wood or a wood-like material, transitioning into a metallic section near the blade. The blade itself is a classic round-point design, slightly curved with a pointed tip, and is constructed from a shiny metal, likely steel. The blade is attached securely to the shaft, with no visible reinforcement ribs or extra features. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with a clean, functional design suitable for digging in soil or loose material. There are no visible brand markings, decorative elements, or signs of wear on the shovel.
description: A long-handled shovel with a dark grip, wooden shaft, and a pointed metal blade. The design is clean, functional, and shows no visible wear or branding.
description_view_0: Side view showing the straight shaft, dark grip, and the thin profile of the pointed metal blade.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the full width and curvature of the round-point metal blade and the straight handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the shaft and the thin edge of the blade.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back curvature of the blade and the straight alignment of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical handle with a dark, textured grip at the top, likely rubber over wood.
attribute_blade_type: Round-point blade with a slight curve and pointed tip, made of metal with a smooth edge.
